question: What is the SGA?
options: System Global Area — shared memory used by all sessions (buffer cache, shared pool, redo log buffer, ...)

explanation.uz: SGA (System Global Area) — instance'ning umumiy (shared) xotirasi: barcha sessiyalar foydalanadi. Ichida buffer cache, shared pool, redo log buffer va boshqalar bor.
explanation.en: The SGA (System Global Area) is the instance's shared memory used by all sessions — it holds the buffer cache, shared pool, redo log buffer, and more.

question: What is the PGA?
options: Program (Process) Global Area — private memory of one server process (sort area, session variables)

explanation.uz: PGA (Program/Process Global Area) — bitta server jarayonining xususiy (private) xotirasi: sort maydoni, sessiya o'zgaruvchilari, kursor holati. Boshqa sessiyalar unga kira olmaydi.
explanation.en: The PGA (Program/Process Global Area) is the private memory of a single server process — sort area, session variables, cursor state. It is not shared with other sessions.

question: The UGA holds session state. In a SHARED server configuration, where does the UGA reside?
options: In the SGA (large pool / shared pool)

explanation.uz: UGA (User Global Area) sessiya holatini saqlaydi. Dedicated server'da u PGA ichida, shared server'da esa SGA ichida (large pool) joylashadi — chunki jarayonlar sessiyalar orasida ulashiladi.
explanation.en: The UGA (User Global Area) holds session state. In a dedicated server it lives in the PGA; in a shared server it moves into the SGA (large pool), because processes are shared across sessions.

question: Which background process performs instance (crash) recovery on startup and cleans up temporary segments?

explanation.uz: SMON (System Monitor) — instance qayta ishga tushganda crash recovery bajaradi va vaqtinchalik segmentlarni tozalaydi, bo'sh joylarni birlashtiradi. Eslatma: bo'sh joyni birlashtirish (coalescing) asosan dictionary-managed tablespace'larга tegishli; hozirgi standart locally-managed tablespace'larда bu kamroq dolzarb.
explanation.en: SMON (System Monitor) performs instance/crash recovery at startup, cleans up temporary segments, and coalesces free space. Note: free-space coalescing mainly applies to dictionary-managed tablespaces; with today's default locally-managed tablespaces it is far less relevant.

question: Which background process cleans up after a failed user process (releases its locks and rolls back its work)?

explanation.uz: PMON (Process Monitor) muvaffaqiyatsiz foydalanuvchi jarayonini tozalaydi: uning loklarini bo'shatadi, tugallanmagan tranzaksiyasini rollback qiladi, resurslarni qaytaradi.
explanation.en: PMON (Process Monitor) cleans up after a failed user process: releases its locks, rolls back its uncommitted transaction, and frees resources.

question: What is the Oracle Listener?
options: A background process inside the instance
A separate network process that receives client connection requests and hands them to a server process

explanation.uz: Listener — instance'dan alohida tarmoq jarayoni. U klient ulanish so'rovlarini qabul qiladi va ularni server jarayoniga topshiradi. Ulanish o'rnatilgach, klient bevosita server jarayoni bilan ishlaydi.
explanation.en: The Listener is a separate network process that receives client connection requests and hands each off to a server process. After the handoff the client talks to the server process directly.

question: Order these storage units from LARGEST to smallest.

explanation.uz: Kattadan kichikka: Tablespace > Segment (masalan jadval) > Extent (uzluksiz bloklar guruhi) > Oracle block (eng kichik I/O birligi). Tablespace bir yoki bir nechta datafile'da yashaydi.
explanation.en: Largest to smallest: Tablespace > Segment (e.g. a table) > Extent (a set of contiguous blocks) > Oracle block (smallest I/O unit). A tablespace lives in one or more datafiles.

question: What is the difference between a DEDICATED and a SHARED server?
options: Dedicated: one server process per client connection; Shared: a pool of server processes serves many clients via dispatchers
Shared server is only for DDL

explanation.uz: Dedicated server'da har klient ulanishiga alohida server jarayoni ajratiladi. Shared server'da esa dispatcher'lar orqali server jarayonlari to'plami ko'p klientga xizmat qiladi — ko'p ulanishda xotira tejaladi.
explanation.en: With a dedicated server, each client gets its own server process. With a shared server, a pool of server processes serves many clients through dispatchers — saving memory under many connections.

question: Which SGA structure caches data blocks read from disk so they can be reused?

explanation.uz: Database buffer cache (SGA ichida) diskdan o'qilgan ma'lumot bloklarini keshlaydi — keyingi murojaatlar diskga bormasdan tez ishlaydi. DBWn 'dirty' bloklarni diskka yozadi.
explanation.en: The database buffer cache (in the SGA) caches data blocks read from disk so later accesses avoid disk I/O. DBWn writes dirty blocks back to disk.

question: Which SGA component caches parsed SQL and execution plans, enabling soft parses?

explanation.uz: Shared pool ichidagi library cache tahlil qilingan SQL va bajarish rejalarini saqlaydi. Bir xil SQL qayta kelsa, soft parse bo'ladi (reja qayta ishlatiladi) — hard parse'dan ancha arzon.
explanation.en: The library cache (in the shared pool) stores parsed SQL and execution plans. If the same SQL returns, it's a soft parse (the plan is reused) — far cheaper than a hard parse.
